name: cbiac-september-10-2025-meeting-summary-and-action-items.pdf
text:
[Page 1]
Charlotte Business INClusion
Advisory Committee Meeting Minutes
Wednesday, September 10, 2025
10:00 a.m. – 11:30 a.m.
I. Attendance: Zahn Patin, Candice Gaddy, Christina Jarrett, Lakesha Womack, Jerome Marez,
Joaquin Soria, Jason Deans, *Jeremy Johnson, Raghu Koth, Gary Beal, Kim Lam, Kym
Gardner,
Absent: Erlan Martinez
II. Welcome: President Patin opened the meeting at 10:01 Candice Gaddy motioned, and
Christina Jarrett seconded the motion.  Zahn also welcomed the newest member Jeremy
Johnson who joins us from the Black Chamber.
III. Stephanie Ayers: Focus Group; CBI staff left  the call and Jasmine the Liaison stayed on to
record. This meeting will be dedicated to the focus group with BBC. The history on the
project between BBC Research and Consulting and the City of Charlotte: BBC is working with
Infrastructure Strategies and the City of Charlotte to assess the capacity of local businesses
and the local workforce in anticipation of an increase in transportation-related work in the
City over the coming years. The BBC is speaking with business owners, representatives of
trade organizations and workforce development programs, and City leadership to
understand the current marketplace and provide re commendations on how the City can
close any gaps or address needs to support equitable and sustainable development. The
purpose of the focus group is to discuss what it is like to start or grow a business, the
challenges and barriers firms like yours face, and your firm's experiences, if relevant,
working with or attempting to work with the City of Charlotte. These insights will contribute
to the BBC's understanding of and documentation on marketplace conditions, as well as
recommendations for the City to consider.
IV. CBI Updates:
• Welcomed Jeremy Johnson as the newest member of CBIAC
• Appreciated Zahn and her willingness and eagerness to step up as
President.
• Still waiting on House Bill 171 and SB 257- which would abolish the HUB.
But we have a basis to continue through the SBE program and will continue

[Page 2]
to serve Minority Business Enterprise (MBE) and Woman Business
Enterprise (WBE). In the process of tweaking who would be considered.
• Annual report is completed but dependent on leadership reviewing it, then
council and then we can share it with CBIAC.
• CBI has two positions that are open- Still deciding if CBI Coordinator
position will be hired for, but ideally by next meeting we will have a new
CBI Compliance position filled.
• We are still CBI but now under the department of Contracting and
Procurement and newly managed by Cheryl Wright who comes to us from
union county will be our new assistant manager. Steve has a good feeling
about her!
• CBI BluPrint is moving smoothy. 8 participating business are really enjoying
and finding value in the program. The launch also went well.
• Contractor development program- impactful but expensive- we are
considering how to move forward to next year- understanding our
limitations but also understanding that the bonding challenges are real.
o Zahn shared that these firms may not understand how these bills
are impacting them! How can we help them be aware of how it
may affect them in real time.
o Julius is meeting with Candice on a weekly meeting and will be
pushing a marketing statedly with CC&M(Charlotte
Communications & Marketing) to make folks aware of this issue
& how this information impacts them.
Subcommittee Share outs
• Economic Development -Christina: The team will be meeting with Danielle Frazier given her
presentation last week.
• Outreach & Engagement -Candice:  A main focus is to make sure that Julius is no longer a team
of 1- Julius has been asked to share his list of upcoming events.
• Impact and Accountability- Jerome met with Steve, Nyema and Jasmine requesting data and
information to support evidence that our work is needed and justified.
V. Adjournment: There being no additional business, Chairman Patin called for a motion
to adjourn. At 11:25 Candice Gaddy motioned & Kim Lam 2nd.
Action Items Assigned To  Due Date
1. Invite Cheryl to next CBIAC Steve & Jasmine  10/8

[Page 3]
2. Julius & Candice keep us updated on the
strategy to support vendors to understand
how these bills impact them.
Julius & Candice 10/8
3. Lina will provide slides and work with Steve &
Nyema to provide more information about
the bills.
Lina/ Steve/ Nyema 10/8
4.  Zahn shared- If you get this question--Is it
still beneficial to get NC HUB certified?
Yes, if you are not certified, please work
to get your NC HUB M/W certifications
for your business. At this time there have
been no official changes to the HUB or CBI
program.
ALL ASAP
5. Utilize CBIAC to host a virtual event to
help vendors understand how these
potential bills affect them and how
certifying as a SBE is beneficial.
ALL ASAP
The action items captured from the Sep 10th, 2025, CBIAC Committee Meeting are as follows:
Sep 10th, 2025, CBIAC Meeting Summary and Action Items submitted by:
Jasmine Timmons   09/10/2025
